title: Standing Against Houthi Aggression Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the US government to officially label the Houthis, a group from Yemen, as a terrorist organization within 90 days. The Houthis are also called Ansarallah and are supported by Iran. The bill also requires the President to punish Houthi members and anyone connected to them by freezing their money and property in the US and banning them from traveling to America. These punishments will also apply to foreign people who work with or are controlled by the Houthis.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Standing Against Houthi Aggression Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these facts. First, on March twenty-first, two thousand seventeen, Reuters reported that Iran, which the US considers a country that sponsors terrorism, sent advanced weapons and military advisers to help Yemen's Ansarallah group. Ansarallah is commonly called the Houthis. Second, on January nineteenth, two thousand twenty-one, the Trump Administration officially labeled Ansarallah as a foreign terrorist organization and a specially designated global terrorist. Third, on February sixteenth, two thousand twenty-one, Secretary of State Blinken removed Ansarallah's designation as a foreign terrorist organization using powers granted under immigration law. Fourth, on March seventh, two thousand twenty-one, Saudi Arabia stopped an Ansarallah drone strike that was aimed at an oil storage yard at Ras Tanura. Fifth, on March nineteenth, two thousand twenty-one, another Ansarallah drone strike hit an oil refinery in Riyadh, which started a fire that was put out. Sixth, after the March nineteenth attack, Ansarallah announced that they had launched six drones at a Saudi Aramco facility and promised to keep attacking Saudi Arabia as long as Saudi Arabia continued what they called aggression against Yemen.

sections.title: Section 3  Designation as Foreign Terrorist Organization and Imposition of Sanctions
sections.text: The Secretary of State must officially label Ansarallah as a foreign terrorist organization within ninety days after this law is passed. This designation will be made under immigration law. The President must also impose penalties on Ansarallah and related people within ninety days after this law is passed. These penalties will apply to Ansarallah itself and any foreign person who is a member, agent, or partner of Ansarallah, or who is owned or controlled by Ansarallah. The penalties include two types of sanctions. First are sanctions that apply to foreign people under Executive Order thirteen thousand two hundred twenty-four, which blocks property and stops transactions with people who commit, threaten to commit, or support terrorism. Second are sanctions described in Executive Order thirteen thousand seven hundred eighty, which protects the nation from foreign terrorist entry into the United States. These sanctions apply to people from Yemen and are based on how the order worked on January nineteenth, two thousand twenty-one.
